pullRequest.title: docs: clarify that v3 major tag doesn't exist, minor version tags do
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #1777

Users familiar with other GitHub Actions expect `pypa/cibuildwheel@v3` to work as a floating major-version tag, but cibuildwheel doesn't provide one. This can be confusing since many popular actions do provide `@v3`-style tags.

This PR adds a note in the **Automatic updates** FAQ section explaining:
- `@v3` major-version tag does not exist
- `@v3.4` minor-version tags **do** exist and auto-receive patch releases
- Dependabot is the recommended approach for staying up to date

As @henryiii noted in the issue: *"Pointing this out more clearly... does sound like a good idea."*

pullRequest.title: Make the `{project}` placeholder available to `repair-wheel-command`
pullRequest.body:
Closes https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/issues/2588
Closes https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/issues/1931

Let me know if you'd prefer a different approach for testing, the change I made amounts to a smoke test.

pullRequest.title: Add support for building Android wheels
pullRequest.body:
* Closes #1960

The corresponding CPython PR, from which the Android Python releases in build-platforms.toml are being generated, is https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/132870.

pullRequest.title: Remove official Appveyor support
pullRequest.body:
Closes #2068

CI hasn't been running for over a year, and their support is unresponsive. Only a handful of our users still use it. This doesn't change any functionality, just removes CI config and documentation.

pullRequest.title: break: remove Travis CI support
pullRequest.body:
close #2290

Stop testing on Travis CI.
References to Travis CI are removed from docs.
Travis CI specifics are kept as-is in cibuildwheel itself (mostly logging stuff).

pullRequest.title: Add TEST_ENVIRONMENT option and set PYTHONSAFEPATH=1 in the test env
pullRequest.body:
See #2358 for discussion.

Here's a draft of this solution. See what you think @henryiii.

Closes https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/issues/2358

pullRequest.title: feat: add a `--clean-cache` command to clean up locations specified at `CIBW_CACHE_PATH`
pullRequest.body:
As described in #2397, this PR adds a `--clean-cache` command to clean `CIBW_CACHE_PATH`. It does not implement granular cache cleans at the moment (it does not clean per supported platform), and `shutil.rmtree()`s the entire folder. I added three tests that account for possible code paths: if cache files exist, if they don't, or if the cache files are somehow in a non-readable/writable location (though I think that aspect should also be validated at the creation time of the cache paths).

This is especially useful for Pyodide builds, as the Emscripten SDK can occupy up to 1.5 GiB of space; however, it is also beneficial for builds on all other platforms.

Closes #2397

pullRequest.title: docs: color output for docs
pullRequest.body: Close https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/issues/2399. A little hacky for now.

pullRequest.title: ci: add labels to autoupdate
Ensure that deep paths exist before copying files.
pullRequest.body:
The OCIContainer handler includes a `copy_file` utility that can copy single files, and directories. It uses `cat` to copy a single file; however, if it is provided a deep path to a file (i.e., `path/to/file.py`), it attempts to copy the file to the destination path without first confirming that the destination path exists.

This PR modifies the containerised `copy_file` implementation to ensure that the target directory exists when copying a deep file reference

Fixes #2405.

pullRequest.title: Revert to running tests from from a temp dir when test-sources is unset
pullRequest.body:
Fix https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/issues/2406.

Alternative to #2419.

This approach restores the v2.x behaviour of running tests from a temp directory. That resolves the issues noted in #2406.

This also removes the ambiguity from https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/pull/2363#issuecomment-2851205233 , so I've removed the restriction that iOS requires test-sources to be set - in this world, we can use the inclusion of the `{project}` placeholder in `test-command` as a signal that users haven't configured things correctly for iOS. Failing that, the `test_fail.py` script that a test runner (pytest) will find can prompt the user to use `test-sources`.

pullRequest.title: fix: rename `gp242` identifier to `gp311_242`
pullRequest.body:
fixes #2410

This normalises identifiers to always start with `{impl}{pyver}`. It's one way of fixing #2410
The rationale behind this solution is while there's only one python version supported by GraalPy for a given version, if PyPy starts to ship a new ABI, we'll probably end-up using `pp311_{ABI_VERSION}` and identifiers will be consistent in between PyPy & GraalPy even if a bit more verbose than needed for GraalPy.

pullRequest.title: feat: support multiple commands on iOS
pullRequest.body:
This should add support for multiple commands on iOS.

Remember to select "hide whitespace" when viewing the diff. :)

Fix #2423.

CC @freakboy3742

pullRequest.title: fix: test-sources should use project dir
pullRequest.body:
Critical fix before 3.0: test-sources should be relative to the project root, not the package directory. The package directory is always a subdirectory of the project directory, so this is more flexible.

By the way, on iOS, it might make sense to expand `{project}` to the relative path? While you normally do know it as well, I'm not sure what all the details are if you run directly on an SDist. (So test-sources work, `{project}`/`cwd()` should probably be the root of the SDist, would be good to verify)

No documentation change since this is how it's described in the docs already. :)

Fix #2436.

pullRequest.title: feat: remove 32-bit linux from auto arch, fix auto32 on linux aarch64
pullRequest.body: This is the proposal (option 2), close #2451.

pullRequest.title: Docs: mobile layout fix
Updates for Pyodide 0.28 release
pullRequest.body:
In account of the new Pyodide release, this PR adds a build identifier for Pyodide 0.28.0, updates the constraints, and updates the docs and tests.

Closes #2485

cc: @hoodmane @ryanking13

pullRequest.title: Bump iOS support package versions.
pullRequest.body:
Bumps the support package revisions for iOS. This includes:

* Python 3.13.5
* Python 3.14.0rc1

There are three notable bug fixes in these updates:
* Changes ensuring that the packages directory in the testbed is treated as a site-packages folder
* Inclusion of iOS shims for `strip`
* Corrections to the handling of `sysconfig.get_paths()` on Python 3.14

Fixes #2494.

pullRequest.title: Clarify language around Python requirements for local `cibuildwheel` usage
pullRequest.body:
First attempt to close #2502.

Clarify that Python installation on Mac and Windows requires needs to be installed directly from https://python.org/downloads and not through other sources like `uv`.

Similarly, it might make sense to have the example usage include a flag that ultimately builds using a container so avoid a similar problem, though I have no strong feelings either way.

Proposed updated quickstart example:
```
# run using uv
uvx cibuildwheel --only cp314-manylinux_x86_64
```

pullRequest.title: BUG: hotfix report defects in logs and HTML summaries
pullRequest.body:
close #2511

disclaimer: I have not attempted to run tests locally yet (I have very limited battery at the moment), but I did run typechecking via `pre-commit`, which correctly caught a mistake in my initial iteration, so I'm reasonably confident in this patch.

pullRequest.title: Move PyPy 3.10 to pypy-eol
pullRequest.body:
Fixes https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/issues/2518.


Should  "PyPy 3.10 v7.3" be remove from the table at https://cibuildwheel.pypa.io/en/stable/#what-does-it-do? cibuildwheel can still target this version, just not by default.

pullRequest.title: Improve docs on how to skip free-threaded builds
fix: ignore empty CIBW_ARCHS
Don't constrain `build-system.requires` with our `dependency-versions`
pullRequest.body:
Fix #2570.

Don't constrain the versions installed during the package build using `dependency-versions`. See #2570 for full discussion. AI summary of the change below

---

This change stops cibuildwheel's dependency-versions from being applied as constraints within the isolated build environment used for the package build. This resolves an issue where conflicts between cibuildwheel's internal requirements and a project's build-system.requires would cause cryptic build failures.

**The Problem**
Currently, on macOS, Windows, and Pyodide platforms, cibuildwheel injects its own dependency constraints (from CIBW_DEPENDENCY_VERSIONS or the defaults) into the isolated environment where a project's build-time dependencies are installed.

This leads to an issue: if a project specifies a build requirement in pyproject.toml (e.g., conan) that has a dependency conflict with cibuildwheel's constraints (e.g., on urllib3), the build fails with an unhelpful subprocess.CalledProcessError traceback.

This behaviour is also inconsistent, as it does not occur on Linux, which invokes the build without injecting these internal constraints.

**The Solution**
Remove the logic that sets the PIP_CONSTRAINT and UV_CONSTRAINT environment variables on the build_env before invoking the build frontend (`pip wheel` or `build`).

The effect is:

- The user's project's build-time dependencies are now resolved independently, without interference from cibuildwheel's toolchain constraints.

- The build process behaviour is now the same across all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows, and Pyodide)

pullRequest.title: Improve information about configuration overriding
pullRequest.body:
Following discussions about the lack of certainty when overwriting build parameters, this PR try to improve the docs to  clarify more. 

https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/discussions/2595#discussioncomment-14424488

closes #2596

pullRequest.title: Update Android test-command handling
pullRequest.body:
In https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/138805 I'm updating the Android testbed so it can take all Python command line options, not just `-c` and `-m`. However, this will be slightly backward incompatible for cibuildwheel, because the `-c` or `-m` must be moved after the `--` separator on the testbed command line. So this PR will need to remain a draft until the next releases of Python 3.13 and 3.14, which are currently scheduled for October 7.

* Fixes #2611

pullRequest.title: Python 3.14 on macOS requires MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET set to 10.15
pullRequest.body: Closes #2612

pullRequest.title: docs: Note the evalution order of environment variables
pullRequest.body: fixes #2616

pullRequest.title: fix: support Docker 29
pullRequest.body: Lowering the case of the keys when reading the json file to support arbitrary `Api`/`API` changes. Fix #2659.

pullRequest.title: Fix the PATH mangling in action.yml
pullRequest.body:
Let's put the `uv` on PATH question aside, and fix #2663.

This change ensures that the PATH entries added by Github when you request `shell: bash` don't make it into the pwsh environment. It also keeps the uv binary on PATH if installed via `extras:`.

Would love to get this fixed soon, then we can figure out the right approach regarding `uv` discovery.

pullRequest.title: Update Python version range to 3.14 in Github Action
pullRequest.body: Fix #2715. I think an upper bound makes sense here as it gives more chance that if a user is pinning an older version of cibuildwheel, it will work.

pullRequest.title: feat: option to build directly with uv
pullRequest.body: Adding an option to build directly with uv. Will conflict with #2321, so probably will rebase after that one.

pullRequest.title: docs: fix TOML reference links
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #2725.

The options docs referenced TOML using `[TOML][]`, but the reference definition was missing, so the site rendered broken links.

- Switch to `[TOML]` and add a `[TOML]: https://toml.io/en/` definition in `docs/options.md`.

pullRequest.title: fix: call `repair-wheel-command` in iOS builds
fix: give uv the full python path
pullRequest.body: Fix #2773. Pass the full path to the directory or Python executable.

pullRequest.title: Print selected build identifiers in preamble before build loop
pullRequest.body:
Users couldn't easily see the full list of planned builds upfront, making it hard to estimate CI timeouts or diagnose cancellations mid-run without scrubbing through logs.

## Changes

- **`cibuildwheel/__main__.py`**: In `print_preamble`, print the count and comma-separated list of build identifiers before "Here we go!", so the full build plan is visible at the top of the log.

Example output:

```
6 builds selected:
cp311-manylinux_x86_64, cp312-manylinux_x86_64, cp313-manylinux_x86_64, cp313t-manylinux_x86_64, cp314-manylinux_x86_64, cp314t-manylinux_x86_64

Here we go!
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[FR] Print out the computed identifier list before looping over them</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Description
> 
> So in my workflows I always set `timeout-minutes` and while working on bumping `cibuildwheel` incrementally, one of the slow jobs (with a 70-minute timeout) was hitting this limit. This is due to the changing defaults scheduling more wheel builds.
> I'd like to be able to estimate how much of the timeout increase it'd need.
> Obviously, I can just try it multiple times but the procedure isn't exactly responsive and means wasting a lot of CI resouces (and personal time invested) — it'd be much nicer if I could make an educated guess and increase that timeout just enough from the first attempt.
> 
> I noticed that `cibuildwheel` prints out something like `info: This container will host the build for cp39-manylinux_aarch64, cp310-manylinux_aarch64, cp311-manylinux_aarch64, cp312-manylinux_aarch64, cp313-manylinux_aarch64, cp314-manylinux_aarch64...` when spawning containers. However, only the first one appears early in the process and the rest need to be waited for and found in the middle of the log. Then, I need to deduce which were still going to be built but haven't started before the cancellation.
> 
> So I figured, if `cibuildwheel` simply prints out the identifiers somewhere in https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/blob/fd6b413212b7f630527a467c1dcc297edb66924d/cibuildwheel/__main__.py#L399-L435, that'd be useful.
> 
> ### Build log
> 
> Example: https://github.com/ansible/pylibssh/actions/runs/23073296178/job/67028557696
> 
> ### CI config
> 
> N/A</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> <comment_new><author>@joerick</author><body>
> Seems reasonable. That print_preamble function already has access to `identifiers`, so it knows already. 
> 
> It could look something like 
> 
>     12 builds selected:
>     cp311-manylinux_x86_64, cp312-manylinux_x86_64, cp313-manylinux_x86_64, cp313t-manylinux_x86_64, cp314-manylinux_x86_64, cp314t-manylinux_x86_64</body></comment_new>
> </comments>
> 


</details>
